payload.title: Unified Engine: focus-not-obscured-header — Focused elements should not be obscured by a sticky header
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `focus-not-obscured-header`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/focus-not-obscured-header`, `/engine/focus-not-obscured-header_success`, `/engine/focus-not-obscured-header_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/focus-not-obscured-header/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** A sticky header remains anchored to the top of the screen while the rest of the page content can be scrolled. If it is not offset from interactive elements, it can overlap and obscure the item in focus.
**Remediation advice:** When a page has a sticky header, make sure that any element receiving focus is not fully covered by the header. One way to achieve this is by using the CSS property scroll-padding-top.
**Fixtures:** 9 pass, 10 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `focus-not-obscured-header` examples published at `/engine/focus-not-obscured-header_success` and `/engine/focus-not-obscured-header_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 9 pass, 10 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

steps.action:
Action:

3. Verify rule title, description, WCAG references, and impact (`serious`) are displayed.
4. Confirm navigation breadcrumbs show Engine Rules → focus-not-obscured-header.
steps.expected_result:

2. Title and description match engine-rules metadata.
3. WCAG references and remediation advice are visible when present.

payload.title: Unified Engine: focus-noticeable — Focused elements should not be obscured by a sticky header
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `focus-noticeable`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/focus-noticeable`, `/engine/focus-noticeable_success`, `/engine/focus-noticeable_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/focus-noticeable/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** All focusable elements must have a visible focus indicator when they receive keyboard focus.
**Remediation advice:** Add a CSS outline or other visual indicator to focusable elements to ensure the currently focused element can be visibly distinguished.
**Fixtures:** 4 pass, 4 fail

2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.

payload.title: Unified Engine: footer-navigation-discernible — Footer navigation should be labelled properly
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `footer-navigation-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/footer-navigation-discernible`, `/engine/footer-navigation-discernible_success`, `/engine/footer-navigation-discernible_failure`

Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.

payload.title: Unified Engine: form-context-change-warning — Interacting with form controls should not cause a change in context unless a user is notified beforehand
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `form-context-change-warning`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/form-context-change-warning`, `/engine/form-context-change-warning_success`, `/engine/form-context-change-warning_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/form-context-change-warning/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Interacting with form controls shouldn't automatically submit a form or cause any other change in context without notifying the user in advance. Form controls that cause a context change on input can disorient a user, since the behavior is not expected.
**Remediation advice:** Make sure that forms can be manually submitted via a submit button, or provide instructions that notify users of the expected behavior before they interact with the control.
**Fixtures:** 5 pass, 8 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `form-context-change-warning` examples published at `/engine/form-context-change-warning_success` and `/engine/form-context-change-warning_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 5 pass, 8 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

4. Confirm navigation breadcrumbs show Engine Rules → form-context-change-warning.
